marketContext: India biscuit market ≈ US$5.5–6.0 bn (~₹50,000 Cr), growing ~6–7% a year. The top 3 players hold >70% (industry estimates vary by report and scope).
disclaimer: Listed companies (Britannia, ITC) show AUDITED financials from their latest full-year results, sourced. Private / D2C groups (Parle, Mondelez India, Unibic, The Whole Truth) use public RoC filings / press; their biscuit-only revenue is ESTIMATED. ALL market-share %s are ESTIMATES from industry reports. Latest available periods: Britannia & ITC FY26 (year ended Mar 2026), Parle/Unibic/The Whole Truth FY25 — private filings lag, so FY25 is their most recent.
shareNote: Market-share % is industry-report-sourced (Statista 2023 base: Britannia ~31% / Parle ~29%, with ITC/others estimated) and is treated as an ESTIMATE for EVERY company, including listed ones — exact share is not audited and moves slowly.

companies.shareSource: Statista — India biscuit companies, 2023 (Britannia ~31%); Britannia retained the lead in FY25/FY26
companies.source: Britannia Industries FY26 full-year results (year ended Mar 2026) — consolidated revenue from operations ₹19,152 Cr, +6.7% YoY; net profit ₹2,537 Cr (AUDITED, announced May 2026)
companies.notes: AUDITED & most-recent full year. Britannia reports a single bakery segment; biscuits are ~75–80% of this (rest: cakes, rusk, bread, dairy, international), so the figure overstates biscuit-only (~₹14,700 Cr) somewhat. Leader by VALUE — Parle leads by volume. The 31% share is an industry-report estimate.

companies.source: ITC FY26 results — FMCG-Others segment ₹24,210 Cr, +10.1% (AUDITED, May 2026); Sunfeast biscuit revenue (~₹5,000 Cr) ESTIMATED — ITC does not break out biscuits
companies.notes: ITC's FMCG-Others segment (₹24,210 Cr FY26) is AUDITED but bundles Aashirvaad, Bingo, Sunfeast, Yippee etc. The Sunfeast biscuit figure (~₹5,000 Cr ≈ 10% of the market), share and YoY are ESTIMATES — ITC discloses no biscuit-only line.

companies.source: Parle Products Pvt Ltd FY25 RoC filing — operational revenue ₹15,568.49 Cr, +8.5% YoY (total income ₹16,191 Cr). Biscuits + confectionery; biscuit split ESTIMATED.
companies.notes: ESTIMATE. Parle Products' FY25 operational revenue (₹15,568 Cr) is RoC-filed but includes confectionery; biscuits (~₹13,200 Cr, ~85%) are the bulk. Volume LEADER — Parle-G is the world's largest-selling biscuit. 29% share is an industry-report estimate.

companies.source: Mondelez India ~US$2 bn revenue (mostly Cadbury chocolate); Oreo / biscuit revenue ESTIMATED
companies.notes: ESTIMATE. India is Oreo's 3rd-largest market, but Mondelez doesn't disclose India biscuit revenue; ~₹2,500 Cr is a rough estimate of the Oreo / biscuit slice of its ~US$2 bn India business. Lotus Biscoff tie-up adds upside.

companies.source: Unibic Foods India Pvt Ltd — FY25 revenue ₹820 Cr (from ₹802 Cr FY24, +2.2%), per RoC filings (Tracxn/ICRA)
companies.notes: ESTIMATE. Cookie-focused, so revenue ≈ biscuit revenue. FY25 ₹820 Cr (RoC); urban-demand softness held growth to ~2%. Share is an estimate.

companies.source: The Whole Truth Foods — revenue ₹216 Cr FY25 (+232% YoY), ₹65 Cr FY24 (RoC filings / Entrackr); biscuit/cookie subset estimated
companies.notes: ESTIMATE — D2C exemplar. Sells protein bars / peanut butter / chocolate AND protein cookies; biscuit-specific revenue is a small subset of the ₹216 Cr total. Explosive growth off a tiny base; represents the emerging D2C protein-biscuit wave.

companies.source: Residual market (industry estimates) — regional & smaller players
companies.notes: ESTIMATE — residual ~23% held by regional & smaller players (Priyagold, Anmol, Bisk Farm, Dukes, Cremica) plus many D2C brands. Shown so the share chart sums to ~100%; excluded from the revenue ranking & classification cards (no clean biscuit-revenue figure).
